sections.title: 5.1 Experiments Setup
sections.originalMarkdown:
**Baselines:** We compare our EcoTune with three baselines: Leveling (RocksDB’s default compaction policy), Lazy Leveling [32], and Moose [56]. Moose is a recently proposed compaction policy that allows each level to have a completely different size ratio and a varying number of sorted runs. Since we do not conduct any compactions at the top level, the data layout of the Lazy Leveling policy closely resembles that of LSM-bush [33]. We implement our EcoTune and Moose using Dostoevsky, a RocksDB-based LSM-tree that already integrates the leveling and lazy leveling policies. We use a two-dimensional vector to represent the data layout after each TMC. The compaction picker in Dostoevsky will determine how to merge sorted runs according to the vector.

**Setup:** Our experiments were conducted on a machine with the following specifications: an AMD EPYC 7742 64-Core Processor, 512MB of L3 cache, and two SSDs (800GB Intel Optane SSD DC P5800X and 3TB D7-P5620 NVMe SSD). The memory write buffer was set to 8MB. A SNARF range filter was constructed for every file with a default memory allocation of 14 bpk. An 8MB block cache was allocated for data blocks. We used 64-bit integer keys from the SOSD dataset [63] and 256 bytes of value for each key. We enabled direct I/Os for both read and write operations. The size ratio for leveling and lazy leveling policy is set to 10. The LSM-tree for leveling and lazy leveling consists of four levels. For Moose², its LSM-tree consists of five levels. Moose allows 3 sorted runs in each non-last level and one sorted run in the last level. The size ratio between level 3 and level 4 (the last level) is 6, while the size ratio between the other levels is 7. In all experiments, there is one client thread to write the in-memory write buffer, one thread for flush, and two threads for TMC. We provide two CPU cores for these threads and only vary the number of cores for MLC and query.

² Moose’s configuration was provided by its authors after we shared our experimental settings with them.

**Workload:** In our experiments, we varied the long range scan ratio in the workload from 0.1 to 1. The long range scan contains Seek + 100Next. The remaining part of the workload contains the same number of Get and Seek operations. Assuming the long range scan ratio is $r$, each operation in the workload have $r$, $\frac{1-r}{2}$, $\frac{1-r}{2}$ probabilities to be a long range scan, Seek operation and Get operation respectively. The targe key is chosen randomly. Without further specification, we fix the write speed to 100 MB/s, which is much higher than the real workload’s speed. This setting is to verify our argument that LSM-tree’s performance is independent of the compaction policy. We use half threads as MLC threads and the other half as query threads by default. A global compaction is triggered when the large level’s size reaches 1/3 of the main level’s size. We use the average throughput of a compaction round to measure the performance of each compaction policy.
sections.translationMarkdown:
**基线：** 我们将 EcoTune 与三个基线进行比较：Leveling（RocksDB 的默认 compaction policy）、Lazy Leveling [32] 和 Moose [56]。Moose 是近期提出的一种 compaction policy，允许每个 level 具有完全不同的大小比率和不同数量的 sorted run。由于我们不在顶层执行任何 compaction，Lazy Leveling policy 的数据布局与 LSM-bush [33] 十分相似。我们使用 Dostoevsky 实现 EcoTune 和 Moose；Dostoevsky 是一种基于 RocksDB 的 LSM-tree，已经集成了 leveling 和 lazy leveling policy。我们使用二维向量表示每次 TMC 后的数据布局。Dostoevsky 中的 compaction picker 将根据该向量决定如何合并 sorted run。

**设置：** 我们的实验在具有以下配置的机器上进行：AMD EPYC 7742 64 核处理器、512MB L3 cache，以及两块 SSD（800GB Intel Optane SSD DC P5800X 和 3TB D7-P5620 NVMe SSD）。内存写缓冲区设为 8MB。我们为每个文件构建 SNARF 范围 filter，默认内存分配为 14 bpk；为数据块分配 8MB block cache。我们使用 SOSD 数据集 [63] 中的 64 位整数键，每个键对应 256 字节的值。读写操作均启用 direct I/O。Leveling 和 lazy leveling policy 的大小比率设为 10。Leveling 和 lazy leveling 的 LSM-tree 均包含四个 level。对于 Moose²，其 LSM-tree 包含五个 level。Moose 允许每个非末层 level 包含 3 个 sorted run，最后一个 level 包含一个 sorted run。Level 3 与 level 4（最后一个 level）之间的大小比率为 6，其他 level 之间的大小比率为 7。在所有实验中，使用一个客户端线程写入内存写缓冲区，一个线程执行 flush，两个线程执行 TMC。我们为这些线程提供两个 CPU 核心，只改变用于 MLC 和查询的核心数量。

² 在我们向 Moose 作者提供实验设置后，其作者为我们提供了 Moose 配置。

**工作负载：** 在实验中，我们将工作负载中的长范围扫描比例从 0.1 改变到 1。长范围扫描包含 Seek + 100Next。工作负载的其余部分包含数量相同的 Get 和 Seek 操作。假设长范围扫描比例为 $r$，则工作负载中的每个操作分别以 $r$、$\frac{1-r}{2}$、$\frac{1-r}{2}$ 的概率成为长范围扫描、Seek 操作和 Get 操作。目标键随机选择。如无特别说明，我们将写入速度固定为 100 MB/s，这远高于现实工作负载的速度。这一设置用于验证我们的论点，即 LSM-tree 的性能独立于 compaction policy。默认情况下，我们将一半线程用作 MLC 线程，另一半用作查询线程。当 large level 的大小达到 main level 大小的 1/3 时，触发 global compaction。我们使用一个 compaction round 的平均吞吐量衡量每种 compaction policy 的性能。

sections.title: 5.2 Experiments on Average Query Throughput
sections.originalMarkdown:
As discussed above, considering the overhead, there are two types of queries: long-range scans and others. We vary the long-range scan ratio from 0.1 to 1. We find that our EcoTune consistently achieves the highest throughput. EcoTune outperforms the Leveling policy by 30% to 150% because EcoTune spends more time on queries rather than compaction while maintaining a moderate number of sorted runs. EcoTune also outperforms the Lazy Leveling policy by 15% to 80% due to having fewer sorted runs for workloads with a high ratio of long-range scans. Our solved compaction policies are shown in Table 5 - Table 8. We found that our EcoTune is also robust to workload shift. The solved policy is not sensitive to different workloads. For long range scan ratio varies from 30% to 80%, the optimal compaction policy remains unchanged. Therefore, workload shift can not easily violate the performance of EcoTune.

*Table 8. 10 cores + 90% - 100% Long Range Scan*

**Impact of Long Range Scan Ratio.** From Figure 7 to Figure 8, we measure the average throughput of different compaction policies with varying long range scan ratios. Our EcoTune achieves up to 1.8x the throughput compared to the second-best algorithm. As the long range scan ratio increases, the throughput of all policies decreases. Previous research indicated that the leveling compaction policy offers the best query performance. In our experiment, we find that the lazy leveling compaction policy and Moose compaction policy outperform the leveling compaction policy in most cases. This is because, during a compaction round, the leveling policy spends too much time on compaction, leaving less time for queries. In contrast, the lazy leveling policy has a slower query speed due to more large sorted runs. Our EcoTune achieves the best performance because it can better balance the time for queries and compactions.

**Impact of CPU Cores.** We use 6 cores and 10 cores (including 2 cores for writing in-memory buffer, flushing, and TMC) to conduct our experiments, respectively. The available cores are used to run both query threads and compaction threads (MLC threads). The query threads are dedicated to queries only. When there are no compaction tasks, the compaction threads also perform queries to achieve higher throughput, as previously illustrated. With 6 cores, there are 5 query threads and 5 MLC threads. With 10 cores, there are 7 query threads and 7 MLC threads. In our experiments, allocating more threads in each case degrades write speed, as excessive threads negatively impact the flush thread. We observe that in all cases, Moose and the lazy leveling compaction policy exhibit similar performance. Our EcoTune consistently achieves the best performance. Furthermore, we find that EcoTune shows a greater advantage with 10 cores compared to 6 cores. This is because EcoTune optimizes the assignment of compactions and queries to threads. However, CPU cores have their own scheduling algorithms to execute different threads concurrently rather than in parallel. This leads to additional CPU contention and context-switching overhead, which is not accounted for in our EcoTune. Such unaccounted overhead is relatively significant with 6 cores.

**Impact of SSD Bandwidth and Latency.** We use an NVMe SSD and an Optane SSD in our experiment. We find that our EcoTune has a greater advantage on the Optane SSD. The reasons are: (1) The Optane SSD has a larger write bandwidth and similar sequential write latency compared to the NVMe SSD. This enables the write speed to scale better, making compaction time relatively low, while the flush time remains similar to that of the NVMe SSD. Since the time of a compaction round is determined by flush time, the Optane SSD allows more time for queries during a compaction round. (2) The random read latency is lower, allowing for more sorted runs on the SSD. This enlarges the design space for the compaction policy.

**长范围扫描比例的影响。** 在图 7 至图 8 中，我们测量了长范围扫描比例不同时各 compaction policy 的平均吞吐量。EcoTune 的吞吐量最高可达到次优算法的 1.8x。随着长范围扫描比例增加，所有 policy 的吞吐量都会下降。以往研究指出，leveling compaction policy 能提供最佳查询性能。在我们的实验中，lazy leveling 和 Moose compaction policy 在大多数情况下优于 leveling compaction policy。这是因为在一个 compaction round 中，leveling policy 在 compaction 上花费了过多时间，留给查询的时间较少。相比之下，lazy leveling policy 由于存在更多 large sorted run，其查询速度较慢。EcoTune 能够更好地平衡查询与 compaction 的时间，因此实现最佳性能。

**CPU 核心数量的影响。** 我们分别使用 6 个核心和 10 个核心（其中包含 2 个用于写入内存缓冲区、flush 和 TMC 的核心）进行实验。可用核心同时用于运行查询线程和 compaction 线程（MLC 线程）。查询线程只执行查询。当没有 compaction 任务时，compaction 线程也会执行查询，以实现更高吞吐量，如前文所述。使用 6 个核心时，有 5 个查询线程和 5 个 MLC 线程；使用 10 个核心时，有 7 个查询线程和 7 个 MLC 线程。在实验中，每种情况下分配更多线程都会降低写入速度，因为过多线程会对 flush 线程产生负面影响。我们观察到，在所有情况下，Moose 与 lazy leveling compaction policy 的性能相近。EcoTune 始终实现最佳性能。此外，我们发现，与使用 6 个核心相比，EcoTune 在使用 10 个核心时优势更大。这是因为 EcoTune 优化了 compaction 和查询到线程的分配。然而，CPU 核心具有自己的调度算法，会并发而非并行地执行不同线程。这会导致额外的 CPU 争用和上下文切换开销，而 EcoTune 并未考虑这些开销。在使用 6 个核心时，这类未纳入考虑的开销相对显著。

**SSD 带宽与延迟的影响。** 实验使用一块 NVMe SSD 和一块 Optane SSD。我们发现 EcoTune 在 Optane SSD 上的优势更大，原因如下：(1) 与 NVMe SSD 相比，Optane SSD 具有更大的写带宽以及相近的顺序写延迟。这使写入速度可以更好地扩展，令 compaction 时间相对较短，而 flush 时间与 NVMe SSD 相近。由于 compaction round 的时间由 flush 时间决定，Optane SSD 能在一个 compaction round 内为查询留出更多时间。(2) 其随机读延迟较低，因此 SSD 上可以存在更多 sorted run。这扩大了 compaction policy 的设计空间。

sections.title: 5.3 Experiments on Latency
sections.originalMarkdown:
When discussing query latency, all previous works [19, 47, 54, 55, 74, 80] focused solely on execution latency. However, we found that the primary contributor to query latency is queuing latency. In real applications, the query request arrival rate is also determined by clients. When the system is temporarily unable to provide sufficient query throughput, incoming query requests will queue up, leading to increased latency. Unfortunately, previous research has not considered this queuing latency. This queuing phenomenon is common in all LSM-trees because compaction consumes significant CPU and I/O resources, which greatly slows down (or even halts) queries during compaction, causing requests to queue.

In this section, we demonstrate how EcoTune reduces query latency (including queuing latency) compared to other compaction policies by achieving higher average query throughput. Under a 100 MB/s write speed, we vary the query arrival speed from 10K/s to 50K/s. As shown in Figure 9, our EcoTune reduces latency by up to 4 and 3 orders of magnitude compared to Leveling and Lazy Leveling, respectively. This is because we spend much less time on compactions compared to the leveling policy, so the average number of accumulated query requests during compactions is relatively low. Our policy also maintains fewer sorted runs, resulting in faster query execution compared to Lazy Leveling. As a result, EcoTune allows most of the accumulated query requests during compaction to be processed quickly. With a query arrival rate of 30K/s, we conducted two experiments. In Figure 9b, queries arrive uniformly, while in Figure 9c, the query arrival rate is set to 15K/s, with each query having a 5% probability of triggering 20 queries simultaneously. Our results show that all algorithms experience increased latency under the workload spike. However, the latency of our EcoTune remains relatively stable, increasing by less than 2 times compared to the uniform case.

We also find that the allocation of threads between MLC threads and query threads significantly impacts query latency. Although allocating all available threads to MLC threads results in higher average query throughput, it is suboptimal for query latency. With more MLC threads, the LSM-tree completes compaction faster, but the query speed during compaction decreases. Conversely, reserving more query threads leads to longer compaction times but higher query speed during compaction. This trade-off is complex, and we are the first to experimentally study this issue.

Given 14 available threads, we vary the number of query threads from 4 to 10. The results are shown in Figure 9. Although our EcoTune is not particularly designed for latency, we find that EcoTune consistently exhibits the lowest latency regardless of thread allocation as it has higher average query throughput³. Each policy experiences relatively high tail latency due to queuing caused by compaction even under a low query arrival rate. In Figure 8, all algorithms achieve a query throughput of more than 10 KOPS. However, in Figure 9a, all algorithms still exhibit a high tail latency of several milliseconds. The reason for this is that when queries arrive as a stream, there are moments when neither queries nor compaction tasks are present, leaving the CPU cores free. At other times, compaction tasks may cause query requests to queue, resulting in higher latency. In other words, the CPU cores for all algorithms are under-utilized. A potential solution to this problem is to adjust the compaction policy based on the query arrival rate to ensure that the CPU cores remain fully utilized. For example, if there are no incoming queries, all available threads can be used to greedily compact sorted runs into the last level. In this scenario, there is no need to reserve CPU or I/O resources for query processing. We propose this query-stream-dependent compaction strategy as an interesting direction for future work.

³ In fact, the queuing latency will finally become infinite if the query arrival speed is higher than the average query throughput. We only measure the tail latency within a compaction round.

*Fig. 9. Latency under different query arrival speeds.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
在讨论查询延迟时，所有以往工作 [19, 47, 54, 55, 74, 80] 都只关注执行延迟。然而，我们发现查询延迟的主要来源是排队延迟。在现实应用中，查询请求的到达速率也由客户端决定。当系统暂时无法提供足够的查询吞吐量时，到达的查询请求会排队，导致延迟增加。遗憾的是，以往研究没有考虑这种排队延迟。所有 LSM-tree 中都普遍存在这种排队现象，因为 compaction 会消耗大量 CPU 和 I/O 资源，在 compaction 期间大幅降低查询速度（甚至使查询完全停止），从而导致请求排队。

本节说明 EcoTune 如何通过实现更高的平均查询吞吐量，相比其他 compaction policy 降低查询延迟（包括排队延迟）。在 100 MB/s 的写入速度下，我们将查询到达速率从 10K/s 改变到 50K/s。如图 9 所示，与 Leveling 和 Lazy Leveling 相比，EcoTune 分别最多将延迟降低 4 个和 3 个数量级。这是因为，相比 leveling policy，我们用于 compaction 的时间少得多，因此 compaction 期间积累的查询请求平均数量相对较少。相比 Lazy Leveling，我们的 policy 还维持了更少的 sorted run，使查询执行更快。因此，EcoTune 能够快速处理 compaction 期间积累的大多数查询请求。在查询到达速率为 30K/s 时，我们进行了两组实验。在图 9b 中，查询均匀到达；而在图 9c 中，查询到达速率设为 15K/s，每个查询有 5% 的概率同时触发 20 个查询。结果表明，所有算法在工作负载尖峰下延迟都会增加。然而，EcoTune 的延迟仍相对稳定，与均匀到达的情况相比增加不到 2 倍。

我们还发现，MLC 线程与查询线程之间的线程分配会显著影响查询延迟。虽然将所有可用线程分配给 MLC 线程能获得更高的平均查询吞吐量，但对查询延迟而言并非最优。MLC 线程越多，LSM-tree 完成 compaction 越快，但 compaction 期间的查询速度会下降。相反，预留更多查询线程会导致 compaction 时间更长，但 compaction 期间的查询速度更高。这一权衡十分复杂，我们是首个通过实验研究该问题的工作。

给定 14 个可用线程，我们将查询线程数量从 4 改变到 10。结果如图 9 所示。虽然 EcoTune 并非专门针对延迟设计，但我们发现，由于它具有更高的平均查询吞吐量，无论如何分配线程，EcoTune 始终具有最低延迟³。即使查询到达速率较低，每种 policy 仍会因 compaction 导致的排队而出现相对较高的尾延迟。在图 8 中，所有算法都实现了超过 10 KOPS 的查询吞吐量。然而在图 9a 中，所有算法仍然表现出数毫秒的高尾延迟。这是因为，当查询以数据流形式到达时，有些时刻既没有查询，也没有 compaction 任务，使 CPU 核心处于空闲状态；而在另一些时刻，compaction 任务可能导致查询请求排队，从而产生更高延迟。换言之，所有算法都没有充分利用 CPU 核心。这个问题的一种潜在解决方案是根据查询到达速率调整 compaction policy，以确保 CPU 核心得到充分利用。例如，如果没有查询到达，可以使用所有可用线程，贪心地将 sorted run compact 到最后一个 level。在这种情况下，无须为查询处理预留 CPU 或 I/O 资源。我们提出，这种依赖查询流的 compaction 策略是一个值得关注的未来研究方向。

³ 事实上，如果查询到达速率高于平均查询吞吐量，排队延迟最终会变为无穷大。我们只测量一个 compaction round 内的尾延迟。

*图 9. 不同查询到达速率下的延迟。*

sections.title: 5.4 Performance under Skewed YCSB workload
sections.titleTranslation: 5.4 倾斜 YCSB 工作负载下的性能
sections.originalMarkdown:
Our previous experiments were conducted with a uniform workload, where nearly all queries required SSD I/Os. In this experiment, we use the YCSB benchmark to generate a skewed workload consisting of 30% long-range scans and 70% point reads. The write speed is set to 100 MB/s. The results, shown in Table 9, demonstrate that EcoTune outperforms lazy leveling by approximately 15%, though this advantage is less pronounced compared to the uniform workload. Conversely, EcoTune’s advantage over the leveling policy is significantly greater, achieving 6× average throughput. The reason is that aggressive compaction frequently invalidates cached data blocks, as reported by [83]. EcoTune uses an aggressive compaction policy at the beginning of a compaction round and the leveling policy is aggressive all the time, which lower the cache hit rate for each algorithm.

| | Optane (KOPS) | NVMe (KOPS) |
|---|---:|---:|
| EcoTune | 174.80 | 122.86 |
| Lazy Leveling | 157.85 | 109.90 |
| Leveling | 29.52 | 23.09 |
| Moose | 142.11 | 104.07 |

*Table 9. Average query throughput on different SSDs with YCSB workload.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
先前实验使用的是均匀工作负载，其中几乎所有查询都需要 SSD I/O。在本实验中，我们使用 YCSB benchmark 生成倾斜工作负载，其中包含 30% 长范围扫描和 70% 点读。写入速度设为 100 MB/s。表 9 所示结果表明，EcoTune 比 lazy leveling 高约 15%，但与均匀工作负载相比，这一优势不那么明显。相反，EcoTune 相对 leveling policy 的优势要显著得多，平均吞吐量达到其 6×。原因在于，如 [83] 所报告，激进 compaction 会频繁使缓存的数据块失效。EcoTune 在 compaction round 开始时采用激进 compaction policy，而 leveling policy 始终保持激进，这会降低各算法的 cache 命中率。

| | Optane（KOPS） | NVMe（KOPS） |
|---|---:|---:|
| EcoTune | 174.80 | 122.86 |
| Lazy Leveling | 157.85 | 109.90 |
| Leveling | 29.52 | 23.09 |
| Moose | 142.11 | 104.07 |

*表 9. YCSB 工作负载下不同 SSD 上的平均查询吞吐量。*

sections.title: 5.5 Performance under High Parallelism and Different Write Speed
sections.titleTranslation: 5.5 高并行度和不同写入速度下的性能
sections.originalMarkdown:
When sufficient CPU resources are available, the average query throughput is bottlenecked by SSD bandwidth. In fact, our model does not differentiate between CPU or I/O bottlenecks. We also conduct experiments at lower write speeds to demonstrate that our EcoTune performs well under various settings.

**High Parallelism Performance.** In Figure 10a and Figure 10b, we use 64 threads to evaluate the performance of different policies under high parallelism. The experiment targets the I/O bottleneck cases, so we assign each thread its own CPU core to avoid any contention on CPU cores. Our EcoTune outperforms all baselines.

**The Impact of Write Speed.** In previous experiments, we fixed the write speed at 100 MB/s, which is significantly higher than most real-world workloads. We are also interested in examining each compaction policy’s query performance when the write speed is lower. We vary the write speed from 20 MB/s to 100 MB/s, with the long-range scan ratio set to 30%. The experimental results are shown in Figure 10c anc Figure 10d. When the write speed is low, the leveling policy achieves higher query throughput than the lazy leveling policy and Moose. A lower write speed means a longer compaction round. For each compaction policy, the compaction time occupies a small portion of the overall compaction round, and the leveling policy has fewer sorted runs and faster query execution. Our EcoTune still identifies the optimal policy and achieves higher query throughput than the leveling policy.

*Fig. 10. (a) and (b) are results of 64 threads (16 MLC threads and 48 query threads) experiments on Optane SSD. (c) and (d) shown query throughput under different write speed. We use 10 cores and the workload contains 30% long range scan.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
当 CPU 资源充足时，平均查询吞吐量的瓶颈是 SSD 带宽。事实上，我们的模型并不区分 CPU 瓶颈和 I/O 瓶颈。我们还在较低写入速度下进行实验，以说明 EcoTune 在各种设置下都表现良好。

**高并行度下的性能。** 在图 10a 和图 10b 中，我们使用 64 个线程评估不同 policy 在高并行度下的性能。实验针对 I/O 瓶颈场景，因此为每个线程分配一个独立的 CPU 核心，以避免 CPU 核心上的任何争用。EcoTune 优于所有基线。

**写入速度的影响。** 在先前实验中，我们将写入速度固定为 100 MB/s，这显著高于大多数现实工作负载。我们还希望考察写入速度较低时每种 compaction policy 的查询性能。我们将写入速度从 20 MB/s 改变到 100 MB/s，并将长范围扫描比例设为 30%。实验结果如图 10c 和图 10d 所示。当写入速度较低时，leveling policy 实现的查询吞吐量高于 lazy leveling policy 和 Moose。较低的写入速度意味着更长的 compaction round。对于每种 compaction policy，compaction 时间在整个 compaction round 中所占比例都较小；而 leveling policy 的 sorted run 更少，查询执行速度更快。EcoTune 仍能识别出最优 policy，并实现高于 leveling policy 的查询吞吐量。

*图 10. (a) 和 (b) 是在 Optane SSD 上使用 64 个线程（16 个 MLC 线程和 48 个查询线程）进行实验的结果。(c) 和 (d) 展示不同写入速度下的查询吞吐量。我们使用 10 个核心，工作负载包含 30% 长范围扫描。*

sections.title: 6 Conclusion
sections.originalMarkdown: We have introduced a novel perspective on the compaction process in LSM-trees. Given a specific write speed, our compaction policy aims to maximize average query throughput. We have observed the competitive relationship between compaction and query operations. To address this, we propose a DP algorithm that optimally balances the competition between compaction and queries.
sections.translationMarkdown: 我们提出了一种看待 LSM-tree compaction 过程的新视角。在给定特定写入速度的情况下，我们的 compaction policy 旨在最大化平均查询吞吐量。我们观察到了 compaction 操作与查询操作之间的竞争关系。为此，我们提出一种 DP 算法，以最优方式平衡 compaction 与查询之间的竞争。
